title: Understanding Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Rotational kinetic energy is given by the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \), where \( I \) is the moment of inertia and \( \omega \) is the angular velocity.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Sphere
text: The moment of inertia \( I \) for a uniform solid sphere is \( I $$=$$ \frac{2}{5} M $$R^2$$ \). Use the given mass and radius of the Earth to calculate \( I \).

title: Calculating Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity \( \omega \) can be calculated using \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{2 \pi}{T} \), where \( T \) is the period of rotation. For Earth's orbit, \( T \) is one year.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Using the formula \( I $$=$$ \frac{2}{5} M $$R^2$$ \), calculate the moment of inertia of the Earth.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Using the formula \( \omega $$=$$ \frac{2 \pi}{T} \), calculate the angular velocity of the Earth in its orbit.

title: Calculate Rotational Kinetic Energy
text: Now, use the formula \( KE_{rot} $$=$$ \frac{1}{2} I \omega**2 \) to find the rotational kinetic energy of the Earth in its orbit.
